We've got LiDAR and GPR showing all these cavities. There's something down there, definitely. CHRIS BLEDSOE: And they're going to tell about it. AJ GENTILE: This year. If it changes everything. A New Era: Energy, Disclosure, and the Exposure of Darkness CHRIS BLEDSOE: Yeah. So I think that'll be part of this new knowledge that's going to happen, going to rewrite the old way. There's a new way, a new knowledge. I think you're going to see fusion energy, helium-3, all of that. Imagine a world tomorrow where Elon doesn't need rockets anymore. AJ GENTILE: But there are going to be forces that are going to fight that every step of the way. CHRIS BLEDSOE: They still are. AJ GENTILE: And they still are. CHRIS BLEDSOE: They just killed the fusion expert at MIT. They just killed it. And they're planning to roll fusion out. You know what that means? About 80% of everything you buy is energy costs. That's right — energy. AJ GENTILE: So what happens if energy's free? CHRIS BLEDSOE: Then we become very wealthy, everything gets very cheap, and living becomes a lot different. AJ GENTILE: And that power structure kind of goes away. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Well, that's part of the new knowledge. The lady said she was going to wake the people up and reveal and expose the darkness. And look at all of what's being exposed right now all around the world — things that people are horrified to see. Dark politics, child trafficking. Whoever thought that going into 2025 we'd be seeing people being trafficked, and the US appears to be the biggest trafficker. AJ GENTILE: It seems so. I think everybody can sense that something's happening, and can sense that there's an ongoing war between good and evil. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Yeah, I think the good is going to prevail and all the evil is going to be exposed. That's my prediction. That's going to happen. I don't think we're going down the road of Armageddon. I think we're going up to a new time of happiness — after all these dark individuals are exposed, kicking and screaming as this all unfolds. AJ GENTILE: I'm going to choose to believe you, Chris. Thank you so much for coming today. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Thank you. The name of your book is UFO of God. AJ GENTILE: UFO of God. And I would say it's an excellent book. Even if you're not into UFOs or religion, it's a fantastic story. It's a great book. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Thank you for that. It's on Amazon and most other outlets. I self-published it. But the crazy thing is that it's been in the top three to five in its categories for three years now. Closing Thoughts and Farewell AJ GENTILE: Well, let's get it up to number one. And when's your next book coming out? CHRIS BLEDSOE: Hopefully by summertime. AJ GENTILE: Now for fans of The Why Files, you know the saying is there's always a book. Something to keep in mind, folks, is that Chris didn't monetize his story for 15 years. CHRIS BLEDSOE: 15 years, right. So I never wrote about it until 2022. This started in 2007. And all the research I've done — I have spent more time out filming the sky than any three people you can come up with. Nobody has probably spent the amount of time I have out observing the sky, and I've done it all on my own dime. And I get people saying, “Oh, you got the horrible camera, you should do this, you should do that.” Why don't you go out and do the filming? AJ GENTILE: Well, let's support his work. Gang, grab that book, and I'll keep everybody posted with what he's doing and how we can support his work. It's very important. I do believe we're in a battle with darkness, and his message is valuable and important. It's a weapon for good, and we all need to wield it. CHRIS BLEDSOE: I appreciate that, AJ. And if things go right in the next 30 or 45 days, we're going to step it up and start an expedition out your way, and hope to have you and Gino come along with us at times. If you want to, we'll be a part of it. We're going to film this every night and get more serious about it. AJ GENTILE: We'll be a part of it. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Yeah. AJ GENTILE: All right, Chris, thanks again. CHRIS BLEDSOE: Thank you, and God bless. AJ's Final Takeaway AJ GENTILE: So we just spent three hours with Chris Bledsoe, and I need to sit with this a minute. Here's what we know. The January 8, 2007 incident at Cape Fear River is documented. Chris, his 17-year-old son, and three adult coworkers were all present, and all of them reported what they saw. MUFON investigated, though Chris says the investigation became a hit job — and that's probably true. What's harder to dismiss is who came next. Harold Povenmire, a NASA engineer who worked on Project Apollo and spent 59 years at the agency, investigated Chris for over a decade and couldn't debunk him. Povenmire died in 2019 still believing. Jim Semivan, a 25-year veteran of the CIA's Clandestine Service, wrote the foreword to Chris's book. Dr. Hal Puthoff from SRI — the man who ran the CIA's remote viewing program — has also studied the case. These aren't fringe characters. These are real people. As for the big claims, Chris says his Crohn's disease was cured after the encounter, and that he has documentation from UNC Chapel Hill showing no markers remain. I couldn't find independent medical verification of that. It's his word and his records. But here's where it gets interesting. Chris claims orbs interact with nuclear sites. Well, that's actually documented elsewhere. There's the 1967 Malmstrom Air Force Base incident, where Captain Robert Salas reported a glowing red object hovering over the facility right before 10 Minuteman nuclear missiles went offline. That's been testified under oath before Congress. UFO activity around nuclear facilities isn't fringe conspiracy — it's in Pentagon records. Now here's what I'm still chewing on. The Lady — this feminine being of light that appears to Chris on Easter — that he connects to Hathor, to Fatima, to divine feminine traditions across cultures. And the 2026 prophecy: when the star Regulus aligns in the gaze of the Sphinx, a new knowledge will come into the world. The astronomy nerds have run the numbers and nobody agrees on the exact date. Some say Easter, some say August, but it's probably going to be October 7th. Chris says he's been saying this since 2012, long before anyone was calculating alignments. So my take is this: Chris Bledsoe is either sitting on experiences that bridge the gap between UFOs, spirituality, and human consciousness in ways we don't have language for yet — or he's the most convincing experiencer in modern history, with an unusual talent for attracting high-level interest. What I can't explain away is the caliber of people who investigated him and walked away believers. NASA engineers, CIA officers, Pentagon officials. Something about this case keeps pulling serious people in, and that's what keeps me thinking about it. Chris's book is UFO of God, available on Amazon. He self-published it and has been in the top five in its category for three years. If you want to go deeper on his story, that's where to start. Until next time — be safe, be kind, and know that you are appreciated. Canadian Pundit Claims ‘Supernatural Forces' Helped USA Win Olympic Gold
Source: Newsweek    Published: 2026-02-23 04:33:00

Published Feb 22, 2026 at 11:33 PM EST Team USA won its first men's hockey gold medal at the Winter Olympic Games in 46 years on Sunday when Jack Hughes slotted home the game-winner in overtime. It was a cinematic moment for the United States of America, which, 16 years earlier, lost to Canada in overtime in the Vancouver Games behind the magic of Sidney Crosby. Canada controlled a majority of the game beginning in the second period, and the Americans held on long enough behind goalie Connor Hellebuyck to push the game to the extra period, where they snatched the victory. While some would call it a hard-fought victory and perseverance, others are claiming other possible reasons for the Americans winning the gold medal. One Canadian sports writer believes that something mystical might be the key to why Team USA won to close out the Olympics. More news: USA Olympic Gold Hero Jack Hughes Linked to Famous Canadian Pop Star More news: USA Hockey Player's Shocking Post-Gold Medal Meal Goes Viral "It was almost as if supernatural forces were at play," Sportsnet writer Luke Fox penned about the gold medal clash. "Storming Team USA's net in hungry waves, Canada was all over the Americans, first tying the airtight gold medal game, then pressing their nemeses like paninis, wanting what's golden." At times, it felt like every small bounce went the United States' way. Although much of that can be attributed to Hellebuyck's phenomenal play, there were some instances in which he would have had no chance of stopping the shot if the Canadians had gotten the puck on target. Most notably, Colorado Avalanche superstar Nathan MacKinnon late in the third period that had a wide open net that he missed. Earlier in the tournament, the best player for Team USA throughout the Olympics, Quinn Hughes, spoke about his possible connection with the spirits. "Sometimes you hear some funny stuff out there, but yeah," he said. "As the people say, I'm usually seeing ghosts, so I'm usually focused on myself." Maybe next time, Canada needs to be more in tune with the ghosts on the ice if they want to walk away with the gold medal. © 2026 Newsweek Digital LLC

A concise essay explores the core reasons there is so much corruption and manipulation in our world and gives inspiring ideas on what we can do to create a brighter future. This thought-provoking piece examines how the primary means for manipulating are through promoting fear, secrecy, and polarization, and through distracting people from their deeper purpose in life. It also provides concrete ways we can turn all of this around. Don't lose hope. This inspiring essay reminds us in a most eloquent way that we were made for these times. Here's one paragraph of this poetic writing. "Ours is not the task of fixing the entire world all at once, but of stretching out to mend the part of the world that is within our reach. Any small, calm thing that one soul can do to help another soul, to assist some portion of this poor suffering world, will help immensely. It is not given to us to know which acts or by whom, will cause the critical mass to tip toward an enduring good." Verifiable information and news on high-level corruption and cover-ups, and the powerful forces creating positive change and transformation. CBS News has no plans to drop health guru Peter Attia from his contributor role after his emails to convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein surfaced last week. Attia was among the 19 contributors named by CBS News Editor in Chief Bari Weiss when she addressed staff about her future plans for the network on Jan. 28. Two days later, Attia showed up in the latest batch of files on Epstein. A Stanford-trained physician who has gained prominence for his expertise in longevity medicine, Attia had a number of email exchanges with Epstein, including a crude discussion about female genitalia. Another message showed Attia expressing dismay that he could not discuss Epstein's activities. “You [know] the biggest problem with becoming friends with you? The life you lead is so outrageous, and yet I can't tell a soul...,” Attia wrote. Conduct such as Attia's association with Epstein would typically be grounds for a network news organization to cut ties with an individual, especially one who is not a full-time employee. Contributors are usually paid by the appearance. But Weiss is said to be opposed to cutting Attia, according to two people familiar with her thinking. As founder of the digital news site the Free Press and as an opinion writer, Weiss spoke out against so-called cancel culture and does not want to be seen as reacting to the Epstein frenzy. Insiders say even if CBS News' ties to Attia are not publicly severed, it's unlikely he will be seen on the air. Note: Don't miss Part 1 and Part 2 of our in-depth investigative series on this massive elite crime ring now coming to light in the documents being made public. For more along these lines, read our concise summaries of news articles on Jeffrey Epstein's trafficking ring.
